But anyways, the point of what this this chart basically shows you is most of times the it'll show you with numbers where your price needs to be based on the size of your facility and how many people you can service. And how much money you want to make, right? And so I just plug these numbers in. Let's say this is where most people come to us, right? Like they can take, let's just say maybe they can take 20 people per session, they're sounding up maybe one ish a week. They're at 110 a month. And these are like kind of the numbers that most people are kind of at somewhere in this range, right? Because this is where their attrition numbers are. Okay, now, my my biggest illustration here is I want to show you how much tiny changes in attrition impact how much money you make over time and why working to get to a 4% 2.5% 2% attrition rate is literally going to make you more money than anything else. Okay, now watch how crazy these numbers get when you start increasing the signups. So the first thing that you're gonna like number one cap is going to be signups. Okay, so you start with your desired income. Let's say you're like, I want to make 40 grand a month. Okay, because I've had people say that they're like, I want to make 40 grand a month, I can only do 20 people capacity and we're signed it five people up, right? So for them to get to 40,000 a month, they have to have like a 1% attrition rate, which is really, really hard. Okay, at that price, right? Now, let's just say they up there, they work with us, they bump their signups. Okay, now to get to 40,000 a month in income, right? That means that they then need to get to 5% attrition rate. But hold on, now they have 10 sessions a day that are running at full capacity. Okay, at 20. Okay, now that tends to like that kind of distribution for class times tends to not work in my experience. Okay, usually getting going over like seven or eight is like that's usually the absolute max that you're going to get. Okay, now, but you remember you still want to make 40,000. So you got two hard numbers to hit. One is that you need to hit 5% attrition, which is challenging, right? And you have an unrealistic like amount of sessions per day to service those people. Okay, so what would you need to do? Like in this instance, right, you need to increase your price. Otherwise, it's not going to happen. You're not going to make 40 grand a month, right? And so let's just say you're at 150. Well, now you can get to 7% okay, and seven sessions a day. Alright, now we have numbers that start to make sense that gives you a goal.
